title: quercus pedunculata subsp. appenina
intro: Quercus pedunculata subsp. appenina, also known as the Appennine oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the Italian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a natural anti-inflammatory agent due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Quercus pedunculata subsp. appenina include its potential to soothe gastrointestinal problems, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves, bark, or roots, which can be used to make teas or tinctures for various medicinal purposes.
